result: The separator coupling's value is a FUNCTION OF DATA DENSITY: R2 gap MF-minus-BLOCK peaks ~0.16 at per=6 (data-scarce, below clique arity ~8), falls to 0.006 at per=40 (rich). MF R2 == DENSE at every (per,seed). Closes the block-vs-info question open since E3: E5's ~0.0002 gap was a data-rich REGIME property, not a method limit.

novelty_classification.note: No new component -- discriminating measurement over existing JunctionTreeCholesky::solve vs solve_block_diagonal. Resolves the F-EVO-5/6/7 open 'non-additive/data-scarce cross-clique target' question via data-scarcity.

guards_added: junction_tree::single_clique_equals_dense extended to cover solve_block_diagonal (§3 coverage gap for E5-added method closed)
framework_impact.open_question_resolved: F-EVO-5/6/7 'a non-additive/data-scarce cross-clique target where BLOCK loses materially and MF/dense hold' -> ANSWERED: data-scarcity, block loses up to 0.16 R2

regression_tests_added: junction_tree::single_clique_equals_dense (now asserts solve_block_diagonal == exact on single clique)
decision_rule_delivered: When to pay for the cross-clique coupling: KEEP it (MF / info form) when data is scarce per clique (< arity) -- worth up to ~0.16 R2; block-diagonal suffices (and is cheaper) when data-rich.
honest_scope: additive-in-features linear target; genuine data-scarcity not a manufactured non-additive coupling
the gap is a (structure, data-density) property not MF-alone
separator-sharing axis and non-additive feature untested

next_experiment_proposal.name: separator-sharing axis (sep, depth) sweep + a genuinely non-additive cross-clique feature; contiguous-storage rewrite + criterion wall-clock
